# Contract: WebRTC Data Channel Messages
*Generated by /speckit-plan — 2026-05-15*

All data channel messages are JSON-encoded strings. Each peer maintains three named data channels with every other peer in the mesh.

---

## Channel Configurations

| Channel Name | `ordered` | `maxRetransmits` | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|
| `presence` | `false` | `0` | Camera position broadcast (unreliable, drop stale) |
| `annotation` | `true` | `3` | Map annotations, state transfer, layer sync |
| `chat` | `true` | `3` | Text chat and pilot mode signals |

The channel that initiates each peer connection is responsible for creating all three named channels. The remote peer accesses them via `RTCPeerConnection.ondatachannel` filtering by `event.channel.label`.

---

## Presence Channel (`presence`)

Broadcast at 5–10hz by every connected peer. Recipients update the local PresenceAvatarManager with the latest state. Stale/out-of-order messages are silently discarded.

```json
{
  "type": "presence",
  "peerId": "peer-uuid",
  "lng": -117.1611,
  "lat": 32.7157,
  "alt": 50000,
  "heading": 0.523,
  "pitch": -1.047,
  "roll": 0.0,
  "pilotPeerId": null,
  "ts": 1716000000000
}
```

| Field | Notes |
|---|---|
| `lng`, `lat` | Degrees (camera position on ellipsoid) |
| `alt` | Meters above ellipsoid |
| `heading`, `pitch`, `roll` | Radians (Cesium camera orientation) |
| `pilotPeerId` | Non-null string when this peer is in pilot mode; null otherwise |
| `ts` | `Date.now()` at send time — used to discard messages older than the last received |

---

## Annotation Channel (`annotation`)

### Add Annotation (any peer → all peers)

Broadcast when a drawing action is completed (mouse release, pin drop).

```json
{
  "type": "annotation-add",
  "annotation": {
    "id": "uuid-v4",
    "annotationType": "stroke",
    "geometry": { "points": [[-117.16, 32.71], [-117.15, 32.72]] },
    "label": null,
    "color": "#ff4444",
    "authorPeerId": "peer-uuid",
    "createdAt": 1716000000000
  }
}
```

**Geometry by `annotationType`**:

```json
// stroke
{ "points": [[lng, lat], ...] }

// line
{ "start": [lng, lat], "end": [lng, lat] }

// circle
{ "center": [lng, lat], "radiusKm": 5.0 }

// rectangle
{ "sw": [lng, lat], "ne": [lng, lat] }

// pin
{ "point": [lng, lat] }
```

### Delete Annotation (any peer → all peers)

```json
{ "type": "annotation-delete", "annotationId": "uuid-v4" }
```

### Clear All Annotations (host only → all peers)

```json
{ "type": "annotation-clear" }
```

Non-host peers ignore this message type.

### Full State Transfer (host → new joiner only)

Sent over a temporary `transfer-state` data channel (`ordered: true, maxRetransmits: 10`) opened by the host to the new peer immediately after the P2P connection is established.

```json
{
  "type": "state-full",
  "annotations": [ /* array of annotation objects, same shape as annotation-add */ ]
}
```

New joiner responds with:
```json
{ "type": "state-ack" }
```

Host closes the `transfer-state` channel after receiving `state-ack` or after a 10-second timeout.

### Layer Sync (host only → all peers)

```json
{
  "type": "layer-sync",
  "fire": true,
  "radio": false,
  "flights": true
}
```

Non-host peers ignore this message type. Recipients update their layer toggle state accordingly.

---

## Chat Channel (`chat`)

### Text Message (any peer → all peers)

```json
{
  "type": "chat",
  "id": "uuid-v4",
  "authorPeerId": "peer-uuid",
  "displayName": "Alice",
  "text": "Moving to the fire perimeter now",
  "ts": 1716000000000
}
```

`text` is clamped to 500 characters before sending. Recipients render in chronological order by `ts`.

### Pilot Mode: Follow Request (any peer → all peers)

```json
{ "type": "pilot-follow", "followerPeerId": "peer-uuid-follower", "targetPeerId": "peer-uuid-pilot" }
```

All peers update their local presence state to reflect the new follower → pilot relationship, triggering sprite cluster recalculation.

### Pilot Mode: Leave (any peer → all peers)

```json
{ "type": "pilot-leave", "followerPeerId": "peer-uuid-follower" }
```

All peers clear the follower's `pilotPeerId`, returning them to independent sprite placement.
